text.tspan: INTUITION: if you've seen a long string of leading zeros in any hash,
you've probably seen MANY items (rare events imply large samples).
Most extreme leading-zero count seen u2248 log2(distinct count)
WHY BUCKETS (not just one counter)?
u2022 averaging across m buckets reduces variance
u2022 each bucket stores ~6 bits (max zeros up to 63)
u2022 total = m * 6 bits u2248 1.5 KB for m=2048; 12 KB for m=16384
u2022 buckets from independent shards can be MERGED (max per bucket)
u2192 distributed counting: each worker computes its HLL, merge at the end
REAL-WORLD USES:
u2022 Google: per-query distinct counting
u2022 BigQuery: APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT() uses HLL++
u2022 Redshift / Snowflake / Presto / Druid: same
u2022 Reddit: distinct page-view counts per post
u2022 Twitter: tweet impression cardinality
u2022 Redis: PFADD / PFCOUNT / PFMERGE commands
1. hash each incoming item h(x)
2. use FIRST p bits of hash to pick a 'bucket' (m = 2^p buckets)
3. count leading zeros of the REMAINING bits
4. store MAX(leading_zeros) seen for that bucket
After the stream:
distinct u2248 alpha * m^2 / sum(2^(-max_zeros[bucket]))
error u2248 1.04 / sqrt(m)
With m=16384 (using 2.5 KB): u00b10.8% error for cardinalities from 0 to billions
